greeting: I walked the whole King's Road to reach this place. Every step was a prayer. And you?
The temple is real. I half believed it was only a story until I saw those golden stones.
Be reverent, traveller. Whatever waits inside is older than any church I've ever prayed in.
quest: I cannot enter. The Guardian turned me back — said I hadn't earned the right. Perhaps you'll fare better. You have a look about you, like the Road has tested you properly.
I'll wait here and tend the camp fire. If you find the Grail... well, I don't know what I expect. Just come back and tell me it's real.
idle: The sunsets here are like nothing I've seen. Gold and rose, as if the sky is blessing this place.
I've met three other pilgrims since I arrived. None of them were allowed to enter.
The Guardian spoke to me with such sadness. I think he's been alone a very long time.
